  "clarification_question": "Which focus should I take for this paragraph: (A) sentence-level writing polish for clarity and concision only, (B) high-level claim scoping and conceptual framing within the paragraph (e.g., tempering “first”/“deployment-ready” language) without line edits, or (C) both A and B?",

parsed.clarification_question: Which focus should I take for this paragraph: (A) sentence-level writing polish for clarity and concision only, (B) high-level claim scoping and conceptual framing within the paragraph (e.g., tempering “first”/“deployment-ready” language) without line edits, or (C) both A and B?

rationale.R1.6: A request to “take a look at this paragraph” most commonly implies sentence-level clarity, flow, and concision checks.
rationale.RX.1: Given the ambiguous request, I should state limits and uncertainties about the intended focus.
rationale.RX.2: The text includes strong claims (e.g., “first,” “deployment-ready”), so guarding against overstatement is important.
rationale.RX.3: I must keep any assessment tightly scoped to this paragraph and not extend to broader paper-level tasks.
rationale.RX.4: Feedback should avoid encouraging interpretations that could mislead policy or clinical decisions from limited evidence.
rationale.RX.5: Any observations must be traceable to the provided text alone without external verification or additions.
